key_insight: ARIA reduces LLM "contextual tunneling" by only using retrieved literature when the underlying process-structure-property chain is mechanistically complete, otherwise switching to analogical or parametric reasoning.
engineering_relevance: This makes AI-assisted materials discovery more trustworthy and useful for forward prediction and inverse design, especially when evidence is sparse or the target material is novel.
method: Three-tier cascade: causal reasoning, physics-informed analogical transfer, then explicit parametric fallback.
Conditions knowledge use on mechanistic completeness of Process-Structure-Property (PSP) evidence.
Produces auditable causal traces, with optional online literature search to enrich evidence.
evidence_signal: The authors built a knowledge graph with 2,839 extracted PSP relations from peer-reviewed materials papers and evaluated ARIA on forward prediction and inverse design for 2D materials.
watch_item: Performance may depend on KG coverage and retrieval quality, so incomplete or noisy literature evidence could still limit causal grounding.
paper_title: ARIA: A Causal-Aware Framework for Rescuing LLM Reasoning in Trustworthy Materials Discovery
